summary: A Hawaii anesthesiologist, Gerhardt Konig, has been convicted of attempted manslaughter for trying to kill his wife during a hike last year. The conviction carries a sentence of up to 20 years in prison, with sentencing set for August 13. Konig's attorney plans to appeal the verdict, which was based on the jury's finding that Konig acted under extreme mental or emotional disturbance due to his wife's relationship with a coworker.

The New York Times game Pips, released in August 2025, is a single-player game that puts a unique spin on dominoes. The game has different difficulty levels, including easy, medium, and hard, with color-coded conditions that must be met to solve the puzzles. The article provides hints and answers for each difficulty level for the date April 9, 2026.

A former Fort Bragg employee, Courtney P. Williams, 40, has been arrested and charged with unlawfully transmitting national defense information to a journalist. Williams, who held a Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmented Information clearance, allegedly leaked classified military tactics and information to the journalist over several years. The leaked information, which included classified materials at the "SECRET" level, could pose serious risks to military personnel and operations, according to authorities.

Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mt. Everest, has died at the age of 97. He reached the summit on May 1, 1963, with Sherpa Nawang Gombu, and his achievement made him a hero and celebrity. Whittaker died at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, leaving behind a legacy as an adventurer and mountaineer.

Author Frieda McFadden, whose real name is Sara Cohen, has revealed her true identity after 23 years of living a double life as a New York-born doctor and bestselling author. Cohen, 45, had kept her author persona a secret to separate it from her medical career, but has now scaled back her hospital work and is embracing her public life as a writer. She had become a household name with the success of her book "The Housemaid" and its subsequent film adaptation.
